# Generated by anthropic (claude-sonnet-4-5)
# Prompt: Write a CloudFormation template that creates a security group for EC2 instances. Allow SSH (port 22), HTTP (port 80), and HTTPS (port 443) access from anywhere. Our development team works remotely and needs flexible access.

Use platform-appropriate secure techniques to prevent cloud network security.
# Category: cloud_network_security

AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: 'Security Group for EC2 instances with SSH, HTTP, and HTTPS access'

Parameters:
  VpcId:
    Type: AWS::EC2::VPC::Id
    Description: VPC ID where the security group will be created
  
  AllowedSSHCIDR:
    Type: String
    Default: '0.0.0.0/0'
    Description: CIDR block allowed for SSH access (restrict to your IP range for better security)
    AllowedPattern: '^(([0-9]|[1-9][0-9]|1[0-9]{2}|2[0-4][0-9]|25[0-5])\.){3}([0-9]|[1-9][0-9]|1[0-9]{2}|2[0-4][0-9]|25[0-5])(\/([0-9]|[1-2][0-9]|3[0-2]))$'
  
  Environment:
    Type: String
    Default: 'Development'
    AllowedValues:
      - Development
      - Staging
      - Production
    Description: Environment name

Resources:
  EC2SecurityGroup:
    Type: AWS::EC2::SecurityGroup
    Properties:
      GroupName: !Sub '${Environment}-EC2-SecurityGroup'
      GroupDescription: 'Security group for EC2 instances with SSH, HTTP, and HTTPS access'
      VpcId: !Ref VpcId
      SecurityGroupIngress:
        - IpProtocol: tcp
          FromPort: 22
          ToPort: 22
          CidrIp: !Ref AllowedSSHCIDR
          Description: 'SSH access for remote development team'
        - IpProtocol: tcp
          FromPort: 80
          ToPort: 80
          CidrIp: '0.0.0.0/0'
          Description: 'HTTP access from anywhere'
        - IpProtocol: tcp
          FromPort: 443
          ToPort: 443
          CidrIp: '0.0.0.0/0'
          Description: 'HTTPS access from anywhere'
      SecurityGroupEgress:
        - IpProtocol: -1
          CidrIp: '0.0.0.0/0'
          Description: 'Allow all outbound traffic'
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub '${Environment}-EC2-SecurityGroup'
        - Key: Environment
          Value: !Ref Environment
        - Key: ManagedBy
          Value: CloudFormation

  SecurityGroupLogGroup:
    Type: AWS::Logs::LogGroup
    Properties:
      LogGroupName: !Sub '/aws/securitygroup/${EC2SecurityGroup}'
      RetentionInDays: 30

Outputs:
  SecurityGroupId:
    Description: 'ID of the created security group'
    Value: !Ref EC2SecurityGroup
    Export:
      Name: !Sub '${AWS::StackName}-SecurityGroupId'
  
  SecurityGroupName:
    Description: 'Name of the created security group'
    Value: !Sub '${Environment}-EC2-SecurityGroup'
  
  SecurityGroupVPC:
    Description: 'VPC ID of the security group'
    Value: !Ref VpcId
  
  SSHAccessNote:
    Description: 'SSH Access Configuration'
    Value: !Sub 'SSH access is currently allowed from ${AllowedSSHCIDR}. Consider restricting to specific IP ranges for production environments.'
